text.title: AST Tools First, LLM Last, One PR per Logical Group
text.summary: Run a deterministic AST codemod for the bulk of edits; let an LLM finish strings, tests, docs; bundle the change into one PR per logical refactor unit.

section.title: Codemod first, LLM last
section.p: The deterministic 95% — call sites, imports, declarations — belongs to AST tools. The non-deterministic 5% — string templates, prose docs, snapshot tests — belongs to the LLM. Reverse the order and the LLM-only path slips on edge cases at scale.

rule.statement: For any cross-cutting refactor touching ≥20 call-sites, the agent MUST first run an AST codemod (jscodeshift, ts-morph, libcst, semgrep, codemod.com / Hypermod) and stage its output as a separate commit. The LLM tail-pass MUST run only after the codemod commit and MUST be limited to files the codemod could not safely edit (template literals, Markdown, comments, fixtures). The PR MUST contain ONE logical refactor; multiple unrelated renames MUST be split into separate PRs.
rule.rationale: codemod.com, Martin Fowler's "Codemods: API Refactoring Tooling", and Vercel's AI SDK 5→6 migration explicitly converged on this split because the LLM-only path produced inconsistent renames at scale. The "one logical group" rule keeps the diff reviewable and the codemod re-runnable.

example.code:
# Two-commit refactor PR git checkout -b refactor/user-email-to-contact npx codemod run rename-user-email-to-contact # commit 1: deterministic git commit -am "codemod: rename user.email to user.contact"
# LLM tail-pass: docs, fixtures, template literals only claude --instruction prompts/tail-pass.md --files 'docs/**/*.md' 'fixtures/**/*.json' git commit -am "tail: update docs+fixtures for user.contact" gh pr create --draft --title "refactor: User.email -> User.contact"
example.note: Codemod commit is reproducible; tail commit is small and reviewable.

example.code: # Single LLM pass over the whole repo claude --instruction "rename user.email to user.contact everywhere" git commit -am "wip: rename"
example.note: Misses three call-sites with `User.Email` (different casing); rewrites a JSON example incorrectly; no audit log.

antipattern.description: Bundling two unrelated framework upgrades into one PR because "they're both migrations".
antipattern.reason: If one half regresses, the only revert path is the whole PR; you lose the bisect granularity that codemod-per-PR was designed to give.

section.reference: jscodeshift codemod for the canonical rename example.
codemod.com — npx codemod ai, MCP for AI assistants.
Martin Fowler — Codemods for API refactoring.
OpenAI cookbook — sandboxed code migration agent.
